children.name: Alectura
children.description: Large, dark-plumaged megapodes with a prominent bare red head and a laterally compressed tail. They are widespread in eastern Australia and are famous for building enormous incubation mounds.

children.name: Alectura lathami
children.description: A common and distinctive bird of eastern Australian rainforests and suburban gardens, easily recognised by its bare red head and yellow throat wattle. Males construct massive compost mounds of leaf litter that generate heat through decomposition, incubating the eggs without direct parental care. Chicks emerge fully feathered and able to fly within hours.

children.name: Leipoa
children.description: A monotypic genus of shy, intricately patterned megapodes that inhabit the arid mallee scrublands of southern Australia. They are renowned for their precisely regulated incubation mounds.

children.name: Leipoa ocellata
children.description: A beautifully camouflaged bird with fine black, white and chestnut scalloping across its back and wings. It builds one of the most sophisticated incubation mounds in the avian world, using a precisely engineered mix of sand and organic matter that the male adjusts daily to maintain a constant 33°C. The species is vulnerable due to habitat loss and introduced predators.

children.name: Megapodius
children.description: A widespread genus of medium-sized, drab-plumaged megapodes found across Southeast Asia, Australasia and the Pacific islands. They are named for their habit of scratching through deep forest litter.

children.name: Megapodius reinwardt
children.description: A dark, chicken-like bird with bright orange legs and a short, erect crest, found from the Lesser Sunda Islands through New Guinea to northern Australia. It inhabits coastal forests and mangrove thickets, where it builds mound nests of sand and vegetation. Its loud, crowing calls echo through the forest at dawn and dusk.

children.name: Megapodius wallacei
children.description: Endemic to the Maluku Islands of Indonesia, this shy megapode prefers the interior of primary rainforest. It has a dark grey body, a brownish crown and pale olive legs. Unlike many megapodes that build mounds, it may lay its eggs in burrows excavated into sun-warmed volcanic soils.

children.name: Megapodius freycinet
children.description: A scrubfowl found in the Moluccas and Raja Ampat Islands.

children.name: Megapodius geelvinkianus
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to Biak Island, Indonesia.

children.name: Megapodius tenebrosus
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to the Tanimbar Islands, Indonesia.

children.name: Megapodius nicobariensis
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to the Nicobar Islands, India.

children.name: Megapodius cumingii
children.description: A scrubfowl found in the Philippines, Borneo, and Sulawesi.

children.name: Megapodius laferrieri
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Sulu Archipelago.

children.name: Megapodius pritchardii
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to Tonga, considered vulnerable.

children.name: Megapodius bernsteinii
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Sula Islands, Indonesia.

children.name: Megapodius leakeyorum
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Sula Islands.

children.name: Megapodius freycinet
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Moluccas and Raja Ampat.

children.name: Megapodius geelvinkianus
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to Biak Island.

children.name: Megapodius tenebrosus
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to the Tanimbar Islands.

children.name: Megapodius nicobariensis
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to the Nicobar Islands.

children.name: Megapodius cumingii
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Philippines and Borneo.

children.name: Megapodius bernsteinii
children.description: A scrubfowl from the Sula Islands.

children.name: Megapodius pritchardii
children.description: A scrubfowl endemic to Tonga.

children.name: Macrocephalon
children.description: A monotypic genus endemic to Sulawesi, instantly recognisable by the prominent helmet-like casque on its head. It is one of the most distinctive and endangered megapodes.

children.name: Macrocephalon maleo
children.description: Sulawesi's most iconic bird, with a black-and-rose-pink body and a distinctive bony casque atop its head. It is unique among megapodes in using solar-heated sand and geothermal-heated soils to incubate its giant eggs, burying them in communal nesting grounds on volcanic beaches and riverbanks. Habitat loss and egg poaching have driven this species to endangered status.

children.name: Eulipoa
children.description: A monotypic genus of megapode endemic to the Moluccas.

children.name: Eulipoa wallacei
children.description: A megapode that lays its eggs in sun-warmed beaches and volcanic soils in the Moluccas.
